title: Lee Crooks (rugby league)

[[Steve Norton]] (brother-in-law) }} }} '''Lee Crooks''' (born 18 September 1963) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s and 1990s and [[Coach (sport)|coach]]ed in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]], [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]] and at club level for [[Hull FC]], [[Western Suburbs Magpies]], [[Balmain Tigers]], [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] and [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]], as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|sr}}, [[Captain (sports)|captain]] of Hull during the [[1985–86 Rugby Football League season|1985–86]] and [[1986–87 Rugby Football League season|1986–87]] seasons,{{cite web|url=https://www.hullfc.com/club/history-stats/past-coaches-captains|title=Coaches and Captains|publisher=hullfc.com|date=31 December 2016|access-date=1 January 2017}} and coached at representative level for [[Serbia national rugby league team|Serbia]], and at club level for [[Keighley Cougars|Keighley]] and [[York Wasps|York]]. ==Background== Lee Crooks was born in [[Kingston upon Hull|Hull]], [[East Riding of Yorkshire]], England. ==Playing career== ===Club career=== ====Hull FC==== Crooks started his career at [[Hull FC]], signing with the club in September 1980, on his 17th birthday. He made his debut in November 1980 against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]],{{cite web |last1=de la Rivière |first1=Richard |title=Rugby League Heroes: Lee Crooks (Part 1) |url=https://www.totalrl.com/rugby-league-heroes-lee-crooks-great-potential-only-partly-fulfilled/ |website=Total Rugby League |access-date=9 April 2023 |date=27 February 2021}} and went on to make 208 appearances for the club.{{cite web|title=December - A Great Month For FC's Hall Of Fame|url=http://www.hullfc.com/news/2015-12-01-december-a-great-month-for-fc-s-hall-of-fame|publisher=Hull F.C.|access-date=19 July 2017|date=1 December 2015}} He also played in three [[Challenge Cup]] Finals with the club. Crooks first cup final was during the [[1981–82 Rugby Football League season|1981–82 season]], when he played {{rlp|sr}} and scored four [[Goal (sport)#Rugby football|goal]]s in Hull's 12–4 victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[1981–82 League Cup (rugby league)|1981–82 John Player Trophy]] Final at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 23 January 1982. Crooks also appeared as a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] (replacing {{rlp|sr}} [[Mick Crane]]) in Hull's 14–14 draw with [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] in the [[1982 Challenge Cup]] Final at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 1 May 1982, in front of a crowd of 92,147, and started at {{rlp|sr}} in the 18-9 victory over Widnes in the [[Replay (sports)|replay]] at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on Wednesday 19 May 1982, in front of a crowd of 41,171.{{cite news|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/complete-history-Hull-FC-s-Challenge-Cup-finals/story-19692285-detail/story.html|title=A complete history of Hull FC's Challenge Cup finals|newspaper=Hull Daily Mail|date=31 December 2013|access-date=1 January 2014|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140203223748/http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/complete-history-Hull-FC-s-Challenge-Cup-finals/story-19692285-detail/story.html|archive-date=3 February 2014|df=dmy-all}} During the [[1982–83 Rugby Football League season|1982–83 season]], Crooks played at {{rlp|sr}}, and scored two [[Goal (sport)#Rugby football|goal]]s and two [[drop goal]]s in Hull's 18–7 victory over [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]] in the [[1982–83 Yorkshire Cup]] Final at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 2 October 1982, and played {{rlp|sr}} in the 14–12 defeat by [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[1983 Challenge Cup]] Final at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 7 May 1983, in front of a crowd of 84,969. Crooks played {{rlp|sr}} in the 13–2 victory over [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in the [[1983–84 Yorkshire Cup]] Final during the [[1983–84 Rugby Football League season|1983–84 season]] at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 15 October 1983. In the [[1984–85 Rugby Football League season|1984–85 season]], Crooks played at {{rlp|pr}} in the 29–12 victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[1984–85 Yorkshire Cup]] Final at [[Boothferry Park]], [[Kingston upon Hull|Hull]] on Saturday 27 October 1984, and played {{rlp|sr}} in the 0–12 defeat by [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[1984–85 League Cup (rugby league)|1984–85 John Player Special Trophy]] Final at [[Boothferry Park]], [[Kingston upon Hull|Hull]] on Saturday 26 January 1985. At the end of the season, he played at {{rlp|pr}}, was [[Captain (sports)|captain]], and scored two [[Goal (sport)#Rugby football|goal]]s in the 24–28 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1985 Challenge Cup]] Final during the [[1984–85 Rugby Football League season|1984–85 season]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 4 May 1985, in front of a crowd of 99,801,{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=445|title=1984–1985 Challenge Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012|archive-date=16 October 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071016210443/http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=445|url-status=dead}} in what is regarded as the most marvellous cup final in living memory,{{cite news|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/100-years-of-rugby-league-from-the-great-divide-to-the-super-era-1597130.html|title=100 years of rugby league: From the great divide to the Super era|newspaper=independent.co.uk|date=20 August 1995|access-date=1 January 2017}} which Hull narrowly lost after fighting back from 12–28 down at half-time. Crooks played {{rlp|sr}}, and scored four [[Goal (sport)#Rugby football|goal]]s in the 24–31 defeat by [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in the [[1986–87 Yorkshire Cup]] Final during the [[1986–87 Rugby Football League season|1986–87 season]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 11 October 1986. ====Western Suburbs==== While at Hull, Crooks spent the [[1985 NSWRL season|1985]] and [[1986 NSWRL season]] with [[Western Suburbs Magpies]]. He scored a try on his debut against [[Penrith Panthers]].{{cite web |last1=de la Rivière |first1=Richard |title=Rugby League Heroes: Lee Crooks (Part 2) |url=https://www.totalrl.com/rugby-league-heroes-lee-crooks-part-2-finding-the-right-rugby-league-role/ |website=Total Rugby League |access-date=9 April 2023 |date=5 March 2021}} In 2010, Crooks was named in the Magpies Team of the Eighties.{{cite web|title=VEST, KEATO, COGGER ALL HONOURED|url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news-display/vest,-keato,-cogger-all-honoured/30925|access-date=30 September 2010|website=weststigers.com.au|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://archive.today/20121230234949/http://www.weststigers.com.au/news-display/vest,-keato,-cogger-all-honoured/30925|archive-date=30 December 2012|df=dmy-all}} ====Leeds==== In June 1987, Crooks was sold to [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] for a world [[Rugby league transfer record|record transfer]] fee of £150,000.{{cite news |title=Crooks moves for £150,000 |url=https://www.britishnewspaperarchive.co.uk/viewer/bl/0003600/19870602/001/0001 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=2 June 1987 |page=1 |via=[[British Newspaper Archive]] |url-access=subscription}} Crooks played {{rlp|pr}} in Leeds' 33–12 victory over [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in the [[1988 Yorkshire Cup]] Final during the [[1988–89 Rugby Football League season|1988–89 season]] at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on Sunday 16 October 1988. During his time at Leeds, Crooks struggled with off-the-field problems and was eventually transfer listed.{{cite news |title=Leeds list Crooks |work=The Guardian |date=31 October 1989 |location=London |page=17 |id={{ProQuest|187094174}} }} ====Castleford==== In January 1990, Crooks was signed by [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] for a fee of £150,000.{{cite news |title=Crooks joins Castleford |work=The Guardian |date=9 January 1990 |location=London |page=15 |id={{ProQuest|187099889}} }} Crooks played at {{rlp|pr}}, scored a [[Goal (sport)#Rugby football|goal]], and was [[Captain (sports)|captain]] in [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]'s 11–8 victory over [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[1990 Yorkshire Cup]] Final during the [[1990–91 Rugby Football League season|1990–91 season]] at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on Sunday 23 September 1990. He missed out on the victory over [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]] in the [[1991 Yorkshire Cup]] Final due to a neck injury.{{cite book |editor-last1=Fletcher |editor-first1=Raymond |editor-last2=Howes |editor-first2=David |url=https://archive.org/details/rothmansrugbylea0000unse_g0s6/page/214/mode/2up |title=Rothmans Rugby League Yearbook 1992-93 |date=1992 |publisher=Headline Publishing Group |location=London |isbn=978-0-7472-7906-8 | page=215}} Crooks played at {{rlp|pr}} and was [[Captain (sports)|captain]] in [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]'s 12–28 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1992 Challenge Cup]] Final during the [[1991–92 Rugby Football League season|1991–92 season]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 2 May 1992, in front of a crowd of 77,386.{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=3066|title=2nd May 1992: Wigan 28 Castleford 12 (Challenge Cup Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2016|access-date=1 January 2017|archive-date=15 February 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170215030604/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=3066|url-status=dead}} He also played in Castleford's 33–2 victory over Wigan in the [[1993–94 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1993–94 Rugby Football League season|1993–94 season]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 22 January 1994.{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/viewreport.php?page=report&id=775|title=Wigan 2 - 33 Castleford|publisher=thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110727175348/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/viewreport.php?page=report&id=775|archive-date=27 July 2011|url-status=dead}} ===Representative honours=== Crooks became the youngest ever Great Britain [[Test match (rugby league)|Test]] forward when he made his début aged 19 on Saturday 30 October 1982. Unfortunately his international début was soured as [[Australian Kangaroos|Australia]] sent shock waves through English football with a 40–4 win at [[Boothferry Park]] in Hull with Crooks providing Britain's only score with 2 penalty goals. He was selected to go on the [[1988 Great Britain Lions tour]]. Crooks won 19 [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] while at Hull in 1982 against Australia (2 matches), in 1984 against France (sub), and Australia (2 matches), in 1985 against New Zealand, and New Zealand (sub), in 1986 against France (2 matches), and Australia (3 matches), in 1987 against France, while at Leeds in 1989 against France, while at Castleford in 1992 against France (2 matches), Papua New Guinea, and he was selected to go on the [[1992 Great Britain Lions tour of Australia and New Zealand]]. He also played in 1994 against France. Crooks also won a [[cap (sport)|cap]] playing at {{rlp|pr}} for [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]] while at Castleford, scoring two [[Goal (sport)#Rugby football|goal]]s in the 17-12 victory over Lancashire at [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]' stadium on 18 September 1991.{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=438|title=Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|date=31 December 2013|access-date=1 January 2014|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120227102536/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=438|archive-date=27 February 2012}} Crooks won a [[Cap (sport)|cap]] for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at Castleford in 1992 against Wales. ==Coaching career== He coached {{rlnt|Serbia}} in their [[2013 Rugby League World Cup qualifying]] campaign. In February 2014, Crooks joined the [[Rugby Football League]] (RFL) again this time in the role of England Regional Performance Coach for the North East. He will oversee the coaching at the RFL's [[North East Academy]], as well as helping to develop community clubs in the region where he intends to build on the great work that was done by his predecessor [[Andy Kelly (rugby league)|Andy Kelly]]. ==Personal life== Crooks married aged 17,{{cite web |title='Mentally and physically it was crazy' - Lee Crooks opens up on his struggles with pressure, teenage stardom and complacency |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/lee-crooks-hull-fc-pressure-4200612 |website=Hull Daily Mail |access-date=9 April 2023 |date=7 June 2020}} but got divorced in 1986. His second wife Karen is the sister of former Hull teammate, [[Steve Norton]]. He is the father of the rugby league footballer; [[Ben Crooks]].{{cite web|url=http://www.hullfc.com/first-team/profile/204599/lee-crooks|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304062145/http://www.hullfc.com/first-team/profile/204599/lee-crooks|url-status=dead|archive-date=4 March 2016|title=Statistics at hullfc.com|publisher=hullfc.com|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *(archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20120216084406/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=218 Profile at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk] *(archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20130612025300/http://hullfc.com/past-players/C Stats → PastPlayers → C at hullfc.com] *(archived by web.archive.org) [https://web.archive.org/web/20160304062145/http://www.hullfc.com/first-team/profile/204599/lee-crooks Statistics at hullfc.com] {{s-start}} {{s-ach|ach}} {{succession box |before = [[Andy Gregory]] |after = [[Garry Schofield]] |title = [[Rugby league transfer record|Rugby League Transfer Record]]
